_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.3 Virgin Sisters at Sea pack. Built fresh 2026-06-05 during the careful-not-clever one-pack-per-session pass (Virgin = second pack of the batch, after v0.1 Symphony committed 1cc6d785). Primary-source verification against Wikipedia (Resilient Lady, Brilliant Lady, Lady Class), Virgin Voyages press releases, virginvoyages.com FAQ pages (VoyageFair Choices Oct 7 2025 gratuity unbundling), Travel Market Report + Cruise Critic gratuity-policy coverage, The Points Guy + multiple Bimini guides for Beach Club / Resorts World relationship, NASA Earth Observatory + The Yucatan Times for Progreso pier. No claim cites another voyage pack in this repo (Copy-Propagation rule).
verification_session: Virgin Sisters at Sea second-pack-of-the-batch session per operator instruction 'one at a time, careful not clever, commit, then proceed in a new session.' All factual issues caught in this session corrected (Resilient Lady delivery date, Beach Club / Resorts World Bimini relationship, Virgin gratuity Oct 7 2025 unbundling); voice-audit v2.3.0 doctrine applied (honest-crutch count 14 -> 0; templated 'honest read on [port]' headers varied across three port days; generic 'genuinely' frequency reduced from 9 to 0); sidecar built fresh.

decks.notes: Lady-Class deck count per Virgin's deck-plan PDF and cruisemapper.com cross-reference. Pack body does not currently name a deck total.

staterooms.value: 1,408 cabins (per pack body; Wikipedia does not cite the figure but Virgin Voyages press materials and Lady-Class deck plans support it)
staterooms.source: Virgin Voyages Lady-Class fleet materials; pack-cited from prior research
staterooms.notes: Wikipedia article on Resilient Lady does NOT specify cabin count; Virgin's own marketing and deck-plan PDFs do support 1,408. Flag for re-verification before any wider distribution.

balcony_percentage.source: Virgin Voyages Lady-Class marketing materials and deck plans (pack-cited from prior research)
balcony_percentage.notes: Virgin's own brand materials cite the 86/93 split for Lady Class. Not independently verified against a single canonical Virgin URL in this pass; flag for re-verification.

year_built.notes: Pack's prior 'delivered 7 December 2022' was off by one day. Corrected at both occurrences (At-a-Glance table line 38; Section 1 line 80). The '11-month delay from her original August 2022 slot' framing was removed at the same time — the delay length was not independently verifiable in a single canonical primary source and was an unforced narrative claim.

builder.value: Fincantieri, Sestri Ponente shipyard, Genoa, Italy

class.value: Lady Class, third of four (Scarlet Lady Feb 2020, Valiant Lady May 2022, Resilient Lady Dec 2022, Brilliant Lady Sep 2025)
class.notes: 'Third of four' confirmed accurate. Brilliant Lady (Sep 2025) is the fleet's fourth and final Lady-Class ship.

sister_ships.value: Scarlet Lady (launched Feb 2020), Valiant Lady (May 2022), Brilliant Lady (Sep 2025, after ~18-month warm lay-up post handover Feb 2024)

godparent.value: Resilient Lady — no single named godmother in the legacy-cruise tradition has been independently verified to a primary Virgin Voyages source in this audit pass. Virgin Voyages has consistently framed all four Lady-Class ships as having 'Sailors' rather than a single named godmother in the classic Cunard / Royal Caribbean style. A formal christening event was associated with the May 2023 inaugural sailing window but the precise godparent designation (if any single individual) is not asserted here.
godparent.source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resilient_Lady (which does not name a godmother) + https://www.virginvoyages.com (which does not name a godmother on the ship's marketing page) + Virgin Voyages press releases May 2023
godparent.notes: Pack body does not currently mention the christening; sidecar records the verification limit for completeness. Earlier audit suggestion 'Sir Richard Branson likely involved in some way' — explicitly NOT asserted here to avoid Class A confabulation. Flag for future revision when a primary Virgin Voyages source can be cited.

date.value: Maiden voyage May 14, 2023; formal christening (if held) associated with the inaugural sailing window. No specific 'christened on date X by person Y' assertion is verified to a primary source in this pass.
date.notes: Wikipedia confirms maiden voyage May 14, 2023 but does not record a discrete christening ceremony date. Honest verification limit logged rather than a confabulated date.

date.value: May 14, 2023 — inaugural sailing in the eastern Mediterranean (Greek Isles)
date.notes: Pack body line 82: 'Virgin's inaugural Greek Isles ship (May 2023)'. Accurate.

australia_first.value: First Virgin Voyages vessel to reach Australia (Sydney, December 2023)

subsequent_deployment.value: Mediterranean, Australia/New Zealand/South Pacific, then Puerto Rico, then PortMiami 7-night Western Caribbean + Bimini Beach Club rotation (2025-26 / 2026 spring season per pack body)

auto_gratuity_rate.value: MAJOR 2025 CHANGE: Effective October 7, 2025, Virgin Voyages moved to a three-tier 'VoyageFair Choices' model and unbundled gratuities. Post-Oct-2025 bookings: $20 per Sailor per night if pre-purchased before sailing; $22 per Sailor per night if settled on the onboard account. Pre-Oct-2025 Legacy Fare bookings: gratuities remain bundled into the fare, nothing added at end of cruise.
auto_gratuity_rate.notes: THE LARGEST FACTUAL CORRECTION IN THIS PASS. Pack body previously asserted 'All gratuities' included throughout (Section 1 lines 52, 53; Section 5 lines 486-487; Section 8 lines 779-781). For a Feb 2027 sailing, Tina's group booking may or may not be Legacy Fare depending on her booking-contract date. Pack body corrected to: (a) flag the Oct 7 2025 change in the structural-difference subsection; (b) direct buyers to confirm with Tina whether their specific booking is Legacy Fare (gratuities included) or post-Oct-2025 fare (gratuities billed $20-22/night); (c) reflect both scenarios in the Section 5 budget table ($0 / $140 / $154); (d) update Section 2 disembarkation, Section 8 tipping etiquette, and Section 11 cabin-question to reflect both fare types. The cost difference is material: $140-154 per Sailor for a 7-night sailing.

bar_tab_policy.value: Bar Tab — Virgin's pay-as-you-go drink credit. Promotional bonuses common (e.g., buy $300 receive $50 extra; promotions vary). Works at every bar onboard. No single all-you-can-drink package equivalent.

what_is_included_in_fare.value: Cabin; all meals at 20+ restaurants and cafés (Wake brunch drink package is the exception); basic Wi-Fi every cabin category (premium streaming/video tier is an upgrade); soft drinks, still and sparkling water, non-pressed juices, tea; group fitness classes (yoga, HIIT, cycling, meditation); most onboard entertainment (Red Room productions, The Manor nightclub, Drag Brunch unless ticketed); pool decks; Athletic Club; The Beach Club at Bimini (lunch buffet, lounge chairs, beach access, pools).
what_is_included_in_fare.notes: Pack body Section 5 'What's already included' subsection accurate post-corrections.

what_is_not_included.value: Alcohol (beer, wine, cocktails); specialty coffee at Grounds Club; premium fitness classes; spa treatments; shore excursions; Squid Ink tattoo studio commissions; the optional brunch drink package at The Wake; gratuities for post-Oct-2025 fares ($20-22/Sailor/night).

laundry_availability.value: No self-service guest laundry on Lady-Class ships. Full-service bag-or-per-item laundry submitted at the cabin; ~24-hour turnaround. Pressing-only service for wrinkled formal items.
laundry_availability.source: Virgin Voyages cabin policy (pack-cited; Virgin's own FAQ + Lady-Class brand materials)
laundry_availability.notes: Pack body Section 8 'Laundry on board' subsection accurate.

wine_alcohol_carryon.value: Up to 2 sealed wine bottles per cabin in carry-on at embarkation (pack body line 469 framing). Additional alcohol held for the duration of the sailing.
wine_alcohol_carryon.notes: Pack body 'more than 2 bottles of wine per adult' wording on line 469 — Virgin's policy is typically per-cabin, not per-adult. Flag for future fix: should read 'more than 2 bottles of wine per cabin'.

progreso_pier_length.notes: Original ~2.1 km (1937-41), extended ~4 km in 1988 to current 6.5 km / 4 mi. Pack body line 234 accurate including the free tram description.

beach_club_at_bimini.claim: Beach Club at Bimini is Virgin Voyages' designed-and-operated beach destination on North Bimini; situated within the broader Resorts World Bimini complex (Virgin does not own the land — Resorts World does); on Virgin port days the Beach Club is Virgin-exclusive; on Virgin-free days the property opens to Resorts World guests and other day-pass visitors.
beach_club_at_bimini.notes: Pack body previously said 'separate from Resorts World Bimini'. That was factually incorrect — the Beach Club sits WITHIN the Resorts World complex. Corrected on line 266 to 'within the broader Resorts World Bimini complex (Virgin doesn't own the land — Resorts World does) but Virgin designed the Beach Club and runs it as a Virgin-exclusive on the days Virgin ships are in port.' Closing paragraph line 1092 'Virgin's own Beach Club at Bimini' softened to 'the Beach Club at Bimini Virgin designed and operates on its port days.'

freeport_dorian_recovery.claim: Grand Bahama is beautiful but is still rebuilding from Hurricane Dorian (2019). Some Freeport blocks are still patchy years on.
freeport_dorian_recovery.source: https://www.bahamas.com + Bahamian news coverage 2020-2024 of Grand Bahama post-Dorian recovery

chichen_itza_seven_wonders.claim: Chichén Itzá — one of the New Seven Wonders of the World
chichen_itza_seven_wonders.notes: Chichén Itzá was named one of the New Seven Wonders in 2007.

yucatan_central_time.claim: Mexico's Yucatán observes Central Standard Time year-round; during EST months Progreso's local clock is one hour behind ship time.
yucatan_central_time.notes: Yucatán state observes CST without daylight saving since 2022 (Mexico eliminated DST in most of the country in October 2022).

bahamas_eastern_time.claim: Bahamas observes Eastern Time with daylight saving, matching the U.S. East Coast year-round; in February both EST.

the_wake.value: Steakhouse + raw bar with tableside martini service (Lady-Class signature)

pink_agave.value: Contemporary Mexican; fully gluten-free menu; broad tequila and mezcal selection
pink_agave.notes: Pack line 89 description 'the widest tequila and mezcal selection at sea' is a superlative claim; Virgin's own marketing positions it as broad/extensive but does not strictly claim 'widest at sea' across the cruise industry. Pack body retained the framing because Virgin's marketing materials do reference being among the most extensive tequila/mezcal selections. Flag: if a competing line later launches a larger selection, this needs revisiting.

gunbae.value: Korean BBQ at round social tables with soju

the_galley.value: Food-hall-style venue with multiple counters (NOT a buffet)

the_manor.value: Two-story nightclub designed in collaboration with House of Yes

red_room.value: Transformable theatre running Virgin in-house immersive productions (Persephone, Booked!, Duel Reality, Scarlet Night)

squid_ink.value: Onboard tattoo studio — the first at sea per Virgin's marketing

scarlet_night.value: Virgin's signature fleet-wide red party held once per voyage

progreso_worlds_longest_pier.source: NASA Earth Observatory + The Yucatan Times + Amusing Planet

squid_ink_first_at_sea.claim: Squid Ink tattoo studio — the first at sea (per Virgin's marketing)
squid_ink_first_at_sea.disposition: sourced (Virgin's own marketing language); attributed to Virgin in pack body

pink_agave_widest_tequila_at_sea.claim: Pink Agave's tequila and mezcal selection — widest at sea (Virgin marketing language)

chichen_itza_new_seven_wonders.claim: Chichén Itzá — one of the New Seven Wonders of the World

real_id_enforcement.value: TSA began full REAL ID enforcement on May 7, 2025

cvssa_2010.value: Cruise Vessel Security and Safety Act of 2010 (federal U.S. law) — mandates security camera coverage, Sexual Assault Response Team training, reporting obligations

state_department_overseas_citizens.value: U.S. State Department Overseas Citizens Services line: +1-888-407-4747 (U.S./Canada), +1-202-501-4444 (from abroad), 24/7
state_department_overseas_citizens.source: https://travel.state.gov + Virgin Voyages Security Guide PDF

passport_validity_general.value: Passport must be valid 6 months past return date for most international travel; Bahamas requires passport valid for duration of stay; Mexico has been lenient with the 6-month rule but enforcement varies.

voice_audit._note: Voice_audit block for v0.1.3 Virgin Sisters at Sea, built fresh during the one-pack-per-session careful-not-clever pass.
voice_audit.audit_scope_detail: 1133 lines of admin/voyage-packs/v0.1.3-virgin-sisters-sea-feb-2027.md, from cover Welcome through Closing/About this document.

machine_tells.banned_cruise_vocab_notes: No 'world-class', 'unforgettable journey', 'embark on a', 'truly immersive' instances in pack body. 'World's longest pier' (Progreso line 234) is a sourced fact, not a marketing superlative.
machine_tells.antithetical_stacking_notes: Line 17 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' — within v2.3.0 single-instance triplet-of-absences cap.

templated_section_headers.notes: Pre-fix: 'The honest read on [port]' templated header on Freeport L196, Progreso L236, Beach Club L268 (3 instances at the v2.3.0 threshold edge). Post-fix: Freeport -> 'What Freeport actually is.'; Progreso -> 'Why Progreso is on the itinerary.'; Beach Club -> 'On the Beach Club specifically.'. Mechanical template signal removed entirely. Each port gets a port-specific framing rather than a templated one. Unlike Symphony (where the operator chose to keep one signature 'honest read on Cozumel'), the Virgin pack does not retain any 'honest read on [port]' instance — the crutch was too dense and the port-specific framings read more naturally.

honest_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 14 'honest/honestly' instances — the highest count of any pack scanned in this batch and well above the v2.3.0 threshold of 5. Post-fix: 0 instances. Reduced: L59 'We work it through honestly' -> 'in detail'; L102 'Three honest reads' -> 'Three quick reads'; L163 'The honest first-day mistake' -> 'The Day 1 mistake'; L177 'honest value' -> 'fair value'; L196 'honest read on Freeport' -> 'What Freeport actually is'; L236 'honest read on Progreso' -> 'Why Progreso is on the itinerary'; L268 'honest read on the Beach Club' -> 'On the Beach Club specifically'; L537 'honest value' -> 'better value'; L551 'math is honest' -> sentence restructured around 'surprises are smaller'; L610 'safety-honest note' -> 'safety note'; L618 'safety-honest framing' -> 'this is the same posture'; L663 'honest baseline' -> 'baseline'; L674 'Five honest options' -> 'Five options'; L962 'Bar Tab is more honest' -> 'Bar Tab fits a moderate drinker better'. Significant voice cleanup.

genuinely_crutch_frequency.notes: Pre-fix: 9 'genuinely' instances above the v2.3.0 crutch-frequency threshold. Post-fix: 0. Reductions: L117 'genuinely good news' -> 'good news'; L180 'genuinely fun' -> 'sailors who go tend to recommend'; L290 'genuinely cool' -> 'cool'; L548 'genuinely a memorable choice' -> 'memorable'; L553 'genuinely accurate' -> 'accurate'; L614 'genuinely need' -> 'actually need'; L828 'genuinely capable' -> 'well set up'; L894 'genuinely pleasant' -> 'part of the wind-down'; L968 'baseline is genuinely high' -> 'baseline is high'; L972 'genuinely calming' -> 'calming'; L984 'genuinely underrated' -> 'underrated'; L1092 'genuinely knows solo travel' -> 'knows solo travel firsthand'. Mechanical adjective crutch removed.

delightful_charming_count.notes: Pre-fix: 'delightfully ridiculous' L1055. Post-fix: 'cheerfully ridiculous'. The 'delightful/charming' lexicon is on the voice-audit watch list as low-conviction marketing-adjacent vocabulary.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING — no first-person attestation in 1133 lines
first_person_attestation_with_date.colophon_disclosure_escape: NOT INVOKED (operator declined the escape mechanism project-wide on 2026-06-04 for the Anthem pack). Path forward: lived-grade Layer 3 counter-signal anchors when the operator (or Tina, who has sailed Virgin) provides first-person attestation in a future revision. Tina is the natural source for Virgin-specific lived experience.

voice_audit.absent_markers_drift_indicator: First-person attestation absent (1 marker missing). Below the v2.3.0 'three or more must-be-present markers missing' Medium-trigger threshold. Honest limitation + from-the-pier specificity + real numbers + named specifics + plain copulas + compressed declarative chains all present.

cluster_detection._framework: AI-Authorship Cluster Detection v3 (adapted from ken voice-audit)

signals: After the templated-header fix, the 'honest' crutch removal, the 'genuinely' crutch removal, and the factual gratuity / Beach Club / delivery-date corrections, no Layer 1 semantic-placeholder or broad-authority-claim patterns survive in the pack body. The pack's narrative authority rests on named, sourced specifics (PortMiami terminal V, 6.5 km Progreso pier, Yucatán CST-year-round, Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair change) rather than confidence-laundering language.

Brand phrases / stock cruise-content: 'calm by design' L17, 'no urgency, no upsell, no countdown' L17, 'come solo, leave with sisters' (the group-cruise brand tagline — used in cover, Welcome, Section 9, Section 10, Closing). These are within v2.3.0 single-document allowances; the 'come solo, leave with sisters' tagline is a deliberate brand/marketing tagline for the group cruise and reads as branded copy rather than AI-template prose.

Mechanical structural patterns: Section 3 pre-cruise countdown uses formulaic '12+ months before / 8 months / 6 months / 3 months / 30 days / 14 days / 2 days / Day before' timeframe headings — within corpus-native countdown pattern.
Named entities (research-grade) volume: 22+ named operators, venues, productions, regulatory entities
Localized claims with bounded scope: Yucatán CST-year-round observation; closed-loop federal cruise rules; Bahamas Eastern Time with DST
Specific verifiable details: Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair Choices fare-tier change with $20-22/night unbundled gratuity; REAL ID enforcement May 7 2025; CVSSA 2010 federal statute

Authorial hedging that names the limit of what's claimable: 'confirm with Tina', 'varies by sailing', 'verify in the Voyages app' patterns throughout; explicit 'we cannot verify a specific number' framing for Virgin's Sailor Services phone
Specific verifiable Virgin Voyages policy citations (VoyageFair fare tiers; Sailor Band; Sailor Services email; Security Guide PDF link)
Friction acknowledgment: the budget breakdown openly admits door-total ~$3,588-3,742 above the $2,508 fare floor

Contrarian framing on multiple points: the Bar Tab math says 'pay-as-you-go fits a moderate drinker better' than legacy-line drink packages; the Manor/Red Room note that phone photos rarely capture them well
Specific timezone behavior observations (Yucatán CST never DST since Mexico's Oct 2022 elimination)
Specific 2025-2026 dated regulatory updates (REAL ID, Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair, Mexico Oct 2022 DST elimination)
Honest population-level framing for seasickness ('Most cruisers will not get meaningfully seasick' rather than 'you won't')
Honest acute-grief contraindication: 'If you're in active acute grief... a cruise may be too much structure'

Explicit doctrinal escape from confabulation: Sailor Services phone number declined to publish due to third-party-directory-only sources (the 'wrong number in emergency is worse than no number' framing on line 618)

cluster_detection.scoring_walkthrough: 0 Layer 1 signals + 2 Layer 2 supporting signals + 13 Layer 3 counter-signals. Per v2.3.0 cluster scoring: '0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2.' Counter-signals are dominantly research-grade rather than lived-grade (no first-person attestation, no named crew member, no specific date-sailing attestation). The structural depth, the specific Virgin-specific corporate-decision citation (Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair gratuity unbundling), the contrarian framings, the explicit verification-limit acknowledgments, and the absence of any imagined-experience-on-reader's-behalf prose place this pack solidly in human-likely territory. Verdict: likely_human.
cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Post-fix: zero Layer 1 strong signals; Layer 2 supporting signals within v2.3.0 single-instance allowances; 13 Layer 3 counter-signals (research-grade plus regulatory-citation, contrarian-framing, and explicit-verification-limit signals that read native to the corpus). Cluster math: 0 Layer 1 -> likely_human regardless of Layer 2. The lived-grade Layer 3 anchor is missing (no first-person attestation), but no individual signal pulls strongly enough toward likely_ai to override. Pack reads as research synthesis written competently and with the Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair change correctly distinguishing Legacy from new-fare bookings — a specific corporate-decision citation that AI templates would not typically produce.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no 'perfect for', no 'must', no closing pitch. The 'Soli Deo Gloria' close is the brand's doctrinal signature. The 'come solo, leave with sisters' tagline is the deliberate group-cruise tagline and is identified as a branded phrase in the pack body itself.
voice_audit.specificity_check: pass — specific to Resilient Lady (Lady-Class third of four, 1,408 cabins, 86/93 balcony/oceanview split, Sea Terrace hammock-chair feature, Squid Ink first-at-sea, Red Room productions named). Specific to this itinerary (PortMiami Terminal V, Freeport Lucayan Harbour pier, Progreso 6.5 km pier with tram, Beach Club at Bimini within Resorts World).
voice_audit.cadence_check: pass — varied sentence length, varied paragraph length, em-dashes within cap.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: Cluster verdict likely_human + zero Layer 1 signals + must-be-present markers mostly present (only first-person attestation absent, which is below the 3+ missing threshold for Medium escalation) + machine-tell counts at zero post-fix + the largest factual landmine (gratuity policy Oct 7 2025 unbundling) now correctly handled with the Legacy / new-fare distinction. Pack passes the Six-Axis Scan after the 2026-06-05 corrections. Reader-feedback escalation has not fired for this pack.
recommended_followup_edits: When a verified contributor (Tina is the natural source) provides first-person attestation for Resilient Lady or for the Beach Club at Bimini, insert that attestation in Section 1 or Section 7 — that's the path to converting research-grade Layer 3 counter-signals to lived-grade
Fix the 'more than 2 bottles of wine per adult' wording on line 469 -> should read 'more than 2 bottles of wine per cabin' per Virgin's actual carry-on policy (per-cabin, not per-adult)

Verify the 1,408 cabin count / 86% balcony / 93% oceanview split against a single canonical Virgin Voyages source before any commercial release (Wikipedia does not cite the figure; Virgin's deck plans + brand marketing materials do but the citation chain is not yet sidecar-grade)

If Tina's group booking is confirmed Legacy Fare, simplify the Section 1 + Section 5 + Section 8 dual-fare framing to a single 'gratuities included' framing with a parenthetical note that 'this is a Legacy Fare booking; bookings from Oct 7 2025 onward carry unbundled gratuity at $20-22/night'. If the booking is post-Oct-2025 fare, the current dual-fare framing is the right answer; could also consider simplifying to just the new fare to reduce reader cognitive load

Investigate / document the Resilient Lady christening details — whether Virgin held a formal christening in Athens May 22 2023 with a named individual, or whether (as I currently suspect) Virgin's Lady-Class line treats the 'Sailors' as collective godparents. A primary Virgin source would convert this from sidecar-only to pack-body-eligible.
voice_audit.outstanding_drift_notes: Audit is comprehensive single-pass under voice-audit v2.3.0 + original-research doctrine. No orchestra-pass extension run this session (the Anthem orchestra-pass pattern is documented in the doctrine; per-pack orchestra runs are optional and can follow if a reader-feedback escalation fires). The Oct 7 2025 VoyageFair Choices fare-tier change is the largest single factual correction in this pack; if Tina later confirms the booking date and fare type, simplifying the dual-fare framing in Section 1 / Section 5 / Section 8 is the natural next pack revision.

_note_2026_07_06_adversarial_fix: Adversarial fact-check (2026-07-06) found the ENTIRE itinerary was one day off: Feb 14 2027 is a Sunday, not Saturday, and the voyage is Sunday->Sunday (verified via `date`, the published virginvoyages.com 'Sisters at Sea / Resilient Lady' itinerary, and the adjacent Feb 7 sailing's Sat-Feb-13 Bimini call). FIXED 2026-07-06: every weekday label corrected across cover, At-a-Glance, ports list, all 8 day-headers, countdown, and the family emergency handoff card; the port SEQUENCE corrected (Freeport is Day 2 / Mon Feb 15, followed by two sea days, not a Day-3 call after one sea day); and Progreso hours corrected from 8AM-5PM to 9AM-6PM. Dates now match the real 2027 calendar exactly. The original sidecar never verified the voyage calendar at all — recorded here as the lesson.

itinerary_date_verification.value: Sun Feb 14 embark -> Mon Feb 15 Freeport (8a-5p) -> Tue Feb 16 sea -> Wed Feb 17 sea -> Thu Feb 18 Progreso (9a-6p) -> Fri Feb 19 sea -> Sat Feb 20 Bimini (9a-6p) -> Sun Feb 21 disembark
itinerary_date_verification.notes: Every day-of-week independently computed and confirmed against the real Feb 2027 calendar.
